revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Isagoda-jinja]][[ja:沙田神社]] {{Expand Japanese|沙田神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Isagoda Shrine | native_name = 沙田神社 | image = Isagoda-jinja keidai.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Isagoda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.221039|137.942556}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Hoori|lt=Hoori|bs|Hoori|en|Hoori|fa|هوری (اسطوره ژاپن)|fr|Hoori|hr|Hoori|it|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|ko|호오리|mni|ꯍꯨꯔꯤ|nl|Ho-ori|no|Hoori|pl|Hoori|pt|Yamasatihiko|ru|Хоори|sh|Hoori|simple|Hoori|sr|Хоори|sv|Hoori|tr|Hoori|uk|Хоорі|vi|Hoori|zh|火折尊|qid=Q1051960|1=Hoori}} | established = {'time': '+0649-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} }} {{nihongo|'''Isagoda Shrine'''|沙田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shinano Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q134917303|ltq=Q134917303|qid=Q134917303|1=San-no-Miya}} ** P1001: {{ill|Q289250|ltq=Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11386164|ltq=Q11386164|qid=Q11386164|1=List of Shikinaisha in Shinano Province}} ** Series ordinal: 5 ** P155: {{ill|Q106852596|ltq=Q106852596|qid=Q106852596|1=Okada Shrine (Matsumoto)}} ** P156: {{ill|Arei Shrine|lt=Arei Shrine|ja|阿禮神社|qid=Q62056170|1=Arei Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q11477019|ltq=Q11477019|qid=Q11477019|1=Shimadachi}} * {{ill|Shinano Province|lt=Shinano Province|ckb|پارێزگای شینانۆ|cs|Provincie Šinano|de|Provinz Shinano|en|Shinano Province|eo|Provinco Ŝinano|es|Provincia de Shinano|fa|ولایت شینانو|fr|Province de Shinano|gl|Provincia de Shinano|hak|Shinano-koet|id|Provinsi Shinano|it|Shinano (provincia)|ja|信濃国|ko|시나노국|lt|Šinano žemė|ms|Wilayah Shinano|nl|Shinano (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Shinano|pt|Província de Shinano|ru|Синано (провинция)|simple|Shinano Province|sk|Šinano (provincia)|sr|Провинција Шинано|th|แคว้นชินาโนะ|tr|Shinano (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сінано|zh_min_nan|Sinano-kok|zh_yue|信濃國|zh|信濃國|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} * {{ill|Q5097503|ltq=Q5097503|qid=Q5097503|1=Chikuma District, Shinano}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.221039|137.942556}} == inception (P571) == * {'time': '+0649-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} == image (P18) == * Isagoda-jinja keidai.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q1051960|ltq=Q1051960|qid=Q1051960|1=Hoori}} * {{ill|Q2329261|ltq=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|1=Toyotama-hime}} * {{ill|Uhijini and Suhijini|lt=Uhijini and Suhijini|es|U-hiji-ni y Su-hiji-ni|ja|ウヒヂニ・スヒヂニ|zh|埿土煑尊與沙土煑尊|qid=Q9090923|1=Uhijini and Suhijini}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181973 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q930545|ltq=Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いさごだじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2100005005874 == Commons category (P373) == * Isagoda-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県松本市島立三ノ宮3316', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Isagoda-jinja ichinotorii.JPG {{commons category|Category:Isagoda-jinja}} [[JA:沙田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11553081}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Isagoda Shrine |Image = [[File:Isagoda-jinja keidai.JPG|270px]]
Precincts (shrine buildings and the second Onbashira) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsumoto City|Q213324|qid=Q213324|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}} 3316 Sannomiya, Shimadate |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2DMS|36|13|15.76|N|137|56|32.79|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Isagoda Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|qid=Q1051960|lt=Hikohodemino-mikoto|1=Hoori}}
{{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Uhijini–Suhijini|Q9090923|qid=Q9090923|lt=Suhijini no Mikoto|1=Uhijini and Suhijini}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (minor)
(trad.) {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
Former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Gōsha and others|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = (trad.) 5th year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|qid=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}} ({{ill|lt=649|Q31117|qid=Q31117|1=649}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other names = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 27|Q2884|qid=Q2884|1=September 27}} |Rituals = Onbashira Festival (once every 6 years) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Isagoda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Isagoda-jinja ninotorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Second torii gate]] '''Isagoda Shrine''' (いさごだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Sannomiya, {{ill|lt=Shimadate (Matsumoto City)|Q11477019|qid=Q11477019|lt=Shimadate|1=Shimadachi}}, {{ill|lt=Matsumoto City|Q213324|qid=Q213324|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} and is traditionally regarded as the {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}; under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Prefectural Shrine|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is the “three-tiered diamond.” === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following three The list of enshrined deities is based on the explanatory plaque within the shrine grounds.. * '''{{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|qid=Q1051960|lt=Hikohodemino-mikoto|1=Hoori}}''' (ひこほほでみのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}''' (とよたまひめのみこと) * '''{{ill|lt=Uhijini–Suhijini|Q9090923|qid=Q9090923|lt=Suhijini no Mikoto|1=Uhijini and Suhijini}}''' (すいじにのみこと) These three deities are considered to belong to the {{ill|lt=Sea deity lineage|UNKNOWN|lt=sea-deity line}} and the {{ill|lt=Amatsukami lineage|Q10939350|qid=Q10939350|lt=heavenly-descendant line|1=Tenson-zoku}}, but this shrine also conducts Suwa-shrine–type rituals that involve erecting Onbashira pillars . There is also a theory that the enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}’s son {{ill|lt=Izasajiri-iri-hiko no Ōji|Q18700560|qid=Q18700560|lt=Izasajiri-iri-hiko no Mikoto|1=Isakiirihikonomiko}} According to *Higashichikuma-gunshi* (as cited in the Isagoda Shrine entry of *Place Names of Nagano Prefecture*).. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, it was originally enshrined at Sagisawa-dake in {{ill|lt=Chikuma District|Q5097503|qid=Q5097503|1=Chikuma District, Shinano}} (present-day Sagisawa, {{ill|lt=Hata Town|Q854697|qid=Q854697|lt=Hata|1=Hata, Nagano}}, Matsumoto City). On June 28 in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|qid=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}} ({{ill|lt=649|Q31117|qid=Q31117|1=649}}), the {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano|1=Shinano Province}} {{ill|lt=provincial governor|Q1351234|qid=Q1351234|1=Kokushi (official)}} first invited the deity and founded the shrine by imperial command Explanatory plaque on the shrine grounds.. Later, during the {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} era ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}}–{{ill|lt=810|Q23819|qid=Q23819|1=810}}), {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|qid=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} is said to have built shrine buildings together with the provincial governor, attributing his success in subjugating bandits on {{ill|lt=Mount Ariake (Azumino City / Matsukawa Village)|Q11516433|qid=Q11516433|lt=Mount Ariake|1=Mount Ariake}} to the divine power of this shrine Village records from the Meiji period identify the figure not as Sakanoue no Tamuramaro but as the Chinju-fu shōgun {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Toshihito|Q3090673|qid=Q3090673|lt=Fujiwara no Ason Toshihito|1=Fujiwara no Toshihito}} (*The Gods of Japan*, Isagoda Shrine entry). . From these traditions, the shrine is thought to have begun by venerating the water spirit of the {{ill|lt=Azusa River|Q11538700|qid=Q11538700|1=Azusa River}} flowing from Hata *The Gods of Japan*, Isagoda Shrine entry., and an inner shrine of this shrine stands at a site said to be the former location at Sagisawa. The present main shrine stands atop ancient jōri-period remains that drew in water from the Azusa River, suggesting it is an old shrine dating back to the initial development of the area . Earthenware and stone tools, as well as iron hoes from the Kofun period and sickles from the medieval and early modern periods, have been excavated from the precincts and are regarded as sacred treasures . === Overview === According to shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} ({{ill|lt=851|Q29966|qid=Q29966|1=851}}) the shrine was constructed by imperial evaluation, and in the third year of Ninju ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|qid=Q30367|1=853}}) it is said to have received a divine rank with Nijō Dainagon Ariyasu as imperial envoy . In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), “Isagoda Shrine” is listed in {{ill|lt=Chikuma District|Q5097503|qid=Q5097503|1=Chikuma District, Shinano}}, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}, and is included among the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. There is also a theory that the “Azusa Water Deity” recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}* entry for March 11 of the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}) refers to this shrine *Place Names of Nagano Prefecture*, Isagoda Shrine entry.. In the late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine buildings were destroyed by warfare between the {{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|qid=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} ({{ill|lt=1321|Q5769|qid=Q5769|1=1321}}–{{ill|lt=1323|Q5771|qid=Q5771|1=1323}}) and {{ill|lt=Shōchū (Japan)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shōchū}} ({{ill|lt=1324|Q5773|qid=Q5773|1=1324}}–{{ill|lt=1325|Q5774|qid=Q5774|1=1325}}) eras, leaving only the main shrine . Thereafter, when Shimadate Ukon built {{ill|lt=Matsumoto Castle|Q739612|qid=Q739612|1=Matsumoto Castle}}, he repaired this shrine as his tutelary deity . Successive lords of Matsumoto Castle valued the shrine as protection against the northeast (the “demon gate”) and donated shrine lands . It is said that the shrine faces east toward Matsumoto Castle for this protective purpose . The shrine has long been regarded as the Sannomiya of Shinano Province and was historically called “San-no-miya” . Even today, it is locally known as “Ubu-no-miya” and is also worshiped as a deity of safe childbirth . {{ill|lt=Hotaka Shrine|Q11597109|qid=Q11597109|1=Hotaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Azumino City|Q534667|qid=Q534667|1=Azumino}} is also considered a Sannomiya. In the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in Meiji 34 ({{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|qid=Q2035|1=1901}}) it was promoted to a {{ill|lt=Prefectural Shrine|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} . In Meiji 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was designated a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku . === Precincts === In addition to the main hall, worship hall, and kagura hall, there is also the Mikoyasu Shrine{{Cite web|Japanese-language book|url=https://www.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/20344.pdf|title=(6) Historic Scenery Seen in the Onbashira Festival|publisher=Matsumoto City|accessdate=2022-09-10}}. The main hall is said to have been constructed in Bunka 5 (1808). File:Isagoda-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall
{{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} File:Isagoda-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Isagoda-jinja karidono.JPG|Temporary hall File:Isagoda-jinja ichinotorii.JPG|First torii gate === Subsidiary and Branch Shrines === === Inner Shrine === The inner shrine is enshrined in Sagisawa, Hata, Matsumoto City. It stands as a stone shrine at a place reached by climbing one of the ravines upstream of the Azusa River. It is said that on Mount Sagisawa there is a huge rock called “Saiden-ishi”. In the past, the selection for the Onbashira Festival was reportedly carried out on Mount Sagisawa, but today it is conducted in the forests of Hata. === Shrines within the Precincts === * Koyasu Shrine, etc. File:Isagoda-jinja keidaisha.JPG|Shrines within the precincts === Festivals === === Periodic Festivals === * {{ill|lt=Onbashira Festival|Q3698846|qid=Q3698846|lt=Shikinen Onbashira Festival|1=Onbashira}} (once every six years, in the Years of the Rabbit and the Rooster)Festival details are based on the explanatory signboard within the precincts. ** Yamadashi Ceremony (Rooster Day in April) ** Hikimawashi (on the day of the annual grand festival) Held once every six years, in conjunction with the {{ill|lt=Annual Festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|lt=Grand Annual Festival|1=Reisai}} in the Years of the Rabbit and the Rooster. Four sacred pillars are cut from the “O-hayashi” forests formerly owned by the old {{ill|lt=Matsumoto Domain|Q1059941|qid=Q1059941|1=Matsumoto Domain}} in Hata, and are hauled out along {{ill|lt=National Route 158|Q6156830|qid=Q6156830|1=Japan National Route 158}}. The erection of the pillars is undertaken as follows: the first pillar by Nakamura and Sannomiya villages; the second by Shimadate Town village and Nagata village; the third by Oba village and Koshiba village; and the fourth by Arai village and Horigome village—each handled by villages of the medieval Shimadate-go district. Unlike other Onbashira festivals in the Matsumoto Basin, this festival follows the style of Suwa Taisha, and the oldest example confirmed in historical records dates to Shotoku 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|qid=Q6873|1=1713}}). Since old times it has been said, “To see people, go to Ichinomiya (Suwa Taisha); to see splendor, go to Ninomiya ({{ill|lt=Ono Shrine|Q11464223|qid=Q11464223|1=Ono Shrine}}); to see costumes, go to Sannomiya,” and elaborate costumes are prepared for each pillar. It is designated as an {{ill|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Q24895900|qid=Q24895900|1=Folk Cultural Property}} by Matsumoto City. === Annual Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival (January 1) * Traffic Safety Prayer Festival (January 3) * Kitchen Deity Festival (January 20) * National Foundation Day Festival / Memorial Service (February 11) * Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival (May 3) * Great Purification (June 30) * Wind Deity Festival (August 27) * Grand Annual Festival (September 26–27) ** Eve Festival (September 26) ** Main Festival (September 27) * Shichi-Go-San Prayer Festival (the nearest Sunday before November 15) * New Grain Prayer Festival (November 23) * Great Purification Festival (December 30) === Cultural Properties === === Matsumoto City–Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Onbashira Festival of Shimadate Isagoda Shrine – Designated June 30, 2000. === On-site Information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsumoto City|Q213324|qid=Q213324|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}} Shimadate Sannomiya 3316 '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Matsumoto Dentetsu|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Matsumoto Dentetsu Kamikochi Line|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikochi Line}} {{ill|lt=Oba Station|Q11435400|qid=Q11435400|1=Ōniwa Station}} (about an 8-minute walk) === Notes === '''Annotations''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanatory signboard * *Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Nagano Prefecture* ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Isagoda Shrine, Matsumoto City * Yoshiro Komatsu, “Isagoda Shrine,” in Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), *Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}})) === External Links === {{Commonscat|Isagoda-jinja}} * [http://takara.city.matsumoto.nagano.jp/city/186.html Onbashira Festival of Shimadate Isagoda Shrine] (Matsumoto City Cultural Properties website) {{Shinto Sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Isagoda Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Isagoda Shrine]][[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:松本市の建築物]] [[Category:松本市の歴史]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Shinano Province]]
